info.description:
The Selling Partner API for Notifications lets you subscribe to notifications that are relevant to a selling partner's business. Using this API you can create a destination to receive notifications, subscribe to notifications, delete notification subscriptions, and more.

For more information, refer to the [Notifications Use Case Guide](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/notifications-api-v1-use-case-guide).
info.title: Selling Partner API for Notifications

post.description:
Sends a mock notification of the specified type to your SQS. The `sendTestNotification` API is grantless. For more information, see "Grantless operations" in the Selling Partner API Developer Guide.

**Usage Plan:**

| Rate (requests per second) | Burst |
| ---- | ---- |
| 1 | 5 |

For more information, see "Usage Plans and Rate Limits" in the Selling Partner API documentation. This is a sandbox-only operation and must be directed to a sandbox endpoint. Refer to [Selling Partner API sandbox](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/the-selling-partner-api-sandbox) for more information.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The request schema for the `sendTestNotification` operation.

parameters.name: notificationType
parameters.description: The type of notification. Refer to [Notification Type Values](../../offline-docs/notification-type-values.md) for supported values and filter rules.

400.description: Request has missing or invalid parameters and cannot be parsed.

errors.details: Invalid Input, recheck that the AppId and DestinationId are valid and DestinationId belongs to the application

403.description: Indicates that access to the resource is forbidden. Possible reasons include Access Denied, Unauthorized, Expired Token, or Invalid Signature.

404.description: The resource specified does not exist.

409.description: The resource specified conflicts with the current state.

413.description: The request size exceeded the maximum accepted size.

415.description: The request payload is in an unsupported format.

429.description: The frequency of requests was greater than allowed.

500.description: An unexpected condition occurred that prevented the server from fulfilling the request.

503.description: Temporary overloading or maintenance of the server.

payloadVersion.description: The version of the payload object to be used in the notification.

testScenario.description: The scenario of the specified notification to be used in the notification payload. If testScenario is empty, a 400 response will be returned back to the developer. The scenarios supported for each notification type can be found in the Selling Partner API Developer Guide.

TestNotification.description: The describer for the test notification that will be delivered.

ErrorList.description: A list of error responses returned when a request is unsuccessful.

code.description: An error code that identifies the type of error that occurred.

message.description: A message that describes the error condition.

details.description: Additional details that can help the caller understand or fix the issue.

Error.description: An error response returned when the request is unsuccessful.

errors.description: One or more unexpected errors occurred during the `sendTestNotification` operation.

SendTestNotificationResponse.description: The response schema for the `sendTestNotification` operation.

destinationId.description: The identifier for the destination where notifications will be delivered.

SendTestNotificationRequest.description: The request schema for the `sendTestNotification` operation.
